title: Where Clause
learning_objective: Use a where clause to compute a value once and reuse it across several guards (the exact BMI rules are in the starter code).
hints: A where block sits under the guards and binds names visible to all of them — define bmi = weight / height ^ 2 there so it's computed once.
Write one guard per threshold, in order (<= 18.5, then <= 25.0, then <= 30.0), with otherwise as the final catch-all.
Return the exact lowercase strings from the starter comment: "underweight", "normal", "overweight", "obese".
LYAH's "Where!?" section in Syntax in Functions covers how where bindings work.
